description: §6.7.7 <request> settled-state machine, FAILURE path (Peter #20 / GitHub #20). Same body-form `<request>` as request-settle-success-rt, but loadUser's route returns a RAW non-2xx (HTTP 500 `{error, detail}` — NOT a scrml `{__scrml_error}` envelope; the __httpError directive models an uncaught host throw / upstream API 500). On settle: `<#userReq>.loading`→false (#loading "false"), `<#userReq>.error` populates so `if=<#userReq>.error` renders #err, `<#userReq>.data` stays absent (#who out of the DOM), and CRITICALLY the parsed error body is NOT written to the @userData success cell (state.userData stays null) — closing the pre-fix type violation where a 500 body was silently treated as success data.
rationale: §6.7.7 settled(failure): .loading→false, .error→caught error, .data retains prior (not, first mount), the success cell is left untouched. §19.9.2: a non-2xx that is NOT a `{__scrml_error}` scrml-fail envelope is a transport/host failure — the per-route stub surfaces it as a rejection so the <request> settles to .error, never the cell. Pre-fix the stub returned resp.json() blindly and the request emitted no transition code, so the 500 body landed in the cell and .error/.loading never moved (Peter #20).
